2606.07054.title: TRACE: Trajectory Reasoning through Adaptive Cross-Step Evidence Aggregation for LLM Agents
2606.07054.paper_summary: Autonomous LLM agents can pursue hidden malicious objectives through sequences of individually benign actions, making sabotage difficult to detect using standard trajectory-level monitoring. Existing approaches either evaluate complete trajectories in a single pass or partition them into independently scored windows, limiting their ability to connect evidence across temporally distant actions. We propose TRACE, a monitoring framework for long-horizon LLM agent trajectories. TRACE operates through a TIJ (Triage-Inspect-Judge) loop that identifies high-signal regions, performs targeted inspection while maintaining accumulated evidence across reasoning steps, and synthesizes a trajectory-level verdict. We evaluate TRACE on ten task domains from SHADE-Arena against state-of-the-art baselines. TRACE achieves an aggregate F1 of 0.713 and recall of 0.844, with the largest gains on tasks requiring long-range evidence linking.
2606.07054.paper_summary_zh: 自主 LLM 代理可以通過一系列個別無害的行動追求隱藏的惡意目標，使得使用標準的軌跡級監控來檢測破壞行為變得困難。現有的方法要麼在單次過程中評估完整的軌跡，要麼將其劃分為獨立計分的窗口，這限制了它們在時間上遙遠的行動之間連接證據的能力。我們提出了 TRACE，一個用於長期 LLM 代理軌跡的監控框架。TRACE 通過 TIJ（分類-檢查-判斷）循環運作，識別高信號區域，在保持推理步驟中累積證據的同時進行針對性檢查，並綜合出一個軌跡級的判決。我們在 SHADE-Arena 的十個任務領域上對 TRACE 進行評估，與最先進的基準相比，TRACE 實現了 0.713 的整體 F1 和 0.844 的召回率，在需要長距離證據鏈接的任務上獲得了最大的增益。
2606.07054.author: Vijitha Mittapalli et.al.
2606.07054.authors: Vijitha Mittapalli, Shreyaa Jayant Dani, Satya Srujana Pilli, Snigdha Ansu, Mohammadreza Teymoorianfard, Franck Dernoncourt, Hongjie Chen, Yu Wang, Ryan A. Rossi, Nesreen K. Ahmed
